title: toxicodendron succedaneum
intro: Toxicodendron succedaneum, also known as the poison sumac, is a plant species belonging to the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its reddish berries and sap that contains urushiol, a highly allergenic oil. The sap of the poison sumac has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been applied to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of poison sumac have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of poison sumac include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
